```python
        lines.append("BY PAIR — LIFETIME")
        lines.append("─" * 40)
        header = f"  {'Pair':<12} {'Trades':<8} {'WR%':<8} {'PnL%':<10} {'AvgWin%':<10} {'AvgLoss%':<10} {'Stops':<7} {'Tgts':<7} {'Time':<7}"
        lines.append(header)
        lines.append("  " + "-" * (len(header) - 2))
        for p, d in sorted(by_pair.items()):
            pw = [x for x in d["pairs_pnls"] if x > 0]
            pl = [x for x in d["pairs_pnls"] if x <= 0]
            aw = sum(pw) / len(pw) if pw else 0
            al = sum(pl) / len(pl) if pl else 0
            wr = d["wins"] / d["trades"] * 100 if d["trades"] else 0
            lines.append(f"  {p:<12} {d['trades']:<8} {wr:<7.1f}% {d['pnl']:<+9.4f}% {aw:<+9.4f}% {al:<+9.4f}% {d['stops']:<7} {d['targets']:<7} {d['timeouts']:<7}")
        lines.append("")

        # ── PER-VERSION PERFORMANCE ──
        versions = {}
        for r in closed:
            raw = json.loads(r["raw_json"]) if r["raw_json"] else {}
            # strategy_version is inside raw_json (the full trade record JSON)
            v = raw.get("strategy_version") or str(r.get("strategy_type", "") or "")
            if not v or v == "None":
                v = "?"
            if v not in versions:
                versions[v] = {"trades": 0, "pnls": [], "stops": 0, "targets": 0, "timeouts": 0}
            versions[v]["trades"] += 1
            pnl = r["pnl_pct"] if r["pnl_pct"] else 0
            versions[v]["pnls"].append(pnl)
            if r["exit_reason"] == "stop_loss":
                versions[v]["stops"] += 1
            elif r["exit_reason"] == "profit_target":
                versions[v]["targets"] += 1
            elif r["exit_reason"] == "time_exit":
                versions[v]["timeouts"] += 1
        lines.append("─" * 40)
        lines.append("PER-VERSION PERFORMANCE")
        lines.append("─" * 40)
        sorted_versions = sorted(versions.keys())
        for i, v in enumerate(sorted_versions):
            vs = versions[v]
            avg = sum(vs["pnls"]) / len(vs["pnls"]) if vs["pnls"] else 0
            wins = [p for p in vs["pnls"] if p > 0]
            wr = len(wins) / len(vs["pnls"]) * 100 if vs["pnls"] else 0
            total = sum(vs["pnls"])
            change = ""
            if i > 0:
                prev_v = sorted_versions[i - 1]
                prev_total = versions[prev_v]["pnls"]
                prev_avg = sum(prev_total) / len(prev_total) if prev_total else 0
                if avg > prev_avg:
                    change = " ▲ improved"
                elif avg < prev_avg:
                    change = " ▼ declined"
                else:
                    change = " → same"
            lines.append(f"  v{v:<4}: {vs['trades']:<3} trades | WR={wr:<5.1f}% | avg={avg:<+9.4f}% | total={total:<+10.4f}% | Stops={vs['stops']} Tgts={vs['targets']} Time={vs['timeouts']}{change}")
        lines.append("")

        # 3. BY PAIR RECENT (last 10)
        lines.append("─" * 40)
        lines.append("BY PAIR — LAST 10 TRADES")
        lines.append("─" * 40)
        for p, d in sorted(by_pair.items()):
            pair_rows = [r for r in closed if r["pair"] == p]
            recent = pair_rows[-10:]
            if not recent:
                continue
            rpnls = [r["pnl_pct"] for r in recent if r["pnl_pct"] is not None]
            rwins = [x for x in rpnls if x > 0]
            rlosses = [x for x in rpnls if x <= 0]
            rwr = len(rwins) / len(recent) * 100 if recent else 0
            rpnl = sum(rpnls) if rpnls else 0
            lwr = d["wins"] / d["trades"] * 100 if d["trades"] else 0
            trend = "improving" if rwr > lwr + 5 else ("declining" if rwr < lwr - 5 else "stable")
            lines.append(f"  {p:<12} {len(recent):<5} trades | WR: {rwr:<5.1f}% | PnL: {rpnl:<+9.4f}% | Trend: {trend}")
            last5 = recent[-5:]
            for t in last5:
                lines.append(f"    {t['pnl_pct']:+.4f}% ({t['exit_reason']})")
        lines.append("")

        # 4. OPEN POSITIONS
        lines.append("─" * 40)
        lines.append("OPEN POSITIONS")
        lines.append("─" * 40)
        if open_json:
            for t in open_json:
                entry = t.get("entry_price", "?")
                ur = t.get("_unrealised_pct", 0)
                held = t.get("hold_cycles", "?")
                stop = t.get("_stop_price", 0)
                target = entry * 1.03 if isinstance(entry, (int, float)) else 0
                stop_dist = ((stop - entry) / entry * 100) if isinstance(entry, (int, float)) and entry and stop else 0
                tgt_dist = ((target - entry) / entry * 100) if isinstance(entry, (int, float)) and entry and target else 0
                pair = t.get("asset", "?")
                lines.append(f"  {pair}: Entry={entry} | Unrealised: {ur:+.4f}% | Held: {held}c")
                lines.append(f"    Stop: {stop} ({stop_dist:+.3f}%) | Target: {target:.5f} ({tgt_dist:+.3f}%)")
        else:
            lines.append("  None")
        lines.append("")

        # 5. CURRENT MARKET STATE (computed live from yfinance)
        lines.append("─" * 40)
        lines.append("CURRENT MARKET STATE")
        lines.append("─" * 40)
        regimes = hb.get("regimes", {})
        chart_ctxs = hb.get("chart_contexts", {}) or {}
        # Only show pairs that belong to this bot (from strategy config or regimes)
        if strat:
            market_pairs = [p for p in ["EUR/USD", "GBP/USD", "AUD/USD", "GBP/JPY", "XAU/USD", "XAG/USD"] if p in strat]
        else:
            market_pairs = list(regimes.keys()) or ["XAU/USD"]
        live_indicators = _fetch_live_indicators(market_pairs)
        for pair_name in market_pairs:
            li = live_indicators.get(pair_name, {})
            rsi = li.get("rsi", "?")
            adx = li.get("adx", "?")
            regime = regimes.get(pair_name, "?")
            bb_pos = li.get("bb_position", "?")
            bb_lower = li.get("bb_lower", "?")
            bb_mid = li.get("bb_mid", "?")
            bb_upper = li.get("bb_upper", "?")
            price = li.get("price", "?")
            chart = chart_ctxs.get(pair_name, "")
            chart_short = (chart[:120] + "...") if len(chart) > 120 else chart
            lines.append(f"  {pair_name}: RSI={rsi} ADX={adx} Regime={regime} BB={bb_pos}")
            if isinstance(bb_lower, float) and isinstance(price, float):
                lines.append(f"    Price={price:.5f} | BB: Lower={bb_lower:.5f} Mid={bb_mid:.5f} Upper={bb_upper:.5f}")
            if chart_short:
                lines.append(f"    Chart: {chart_short}")
        lines.append("")

        # 6. STRATEGY PARAMETERS
        lines.append("─" * 40)
        lines.append("STRATEGY PARAMETERS")
        lines.append("─" * 40)
        for pair_name, s in strat.items():
            if not isinstance(s, dict):
                continue
            st = s.get("strategy_type", "?")
            ver = s.get("version", "?")
            entry = s.get("entry", {})
            thresh = entry.get("mr_entry_rsi") or entry.get("threshold", "?")
            sl = s.get("stop_loss_pct", "?")
            pt = s.get("profit_target_pct", "?")
            atr_floor = s.get("use_atr_floor", "?")
            bear = s.get("allow_bear_entries", False)
            lines.append(f"  {pair_name}: {st} v{ver} | Thresh={thresh} | Stop={sl}% | Target={pt}% | Floor={atr_floor} | Bear={bear}")
        lines.append("")

        # 7. REFLECTIONS
        hyps = conn.execute("SELECT * FROM hypotheses WHERE bot=? ORDER BY ts DESC", (bot,)).fetchall()
        if valid_pairs:
            hyps = [h for h in hyps if h["pair"] in valid_pairs]
        if hyps:
            lines.append("─" * 40)
            lines.append("REFLECTIONS")
            lines.append("─" * 40)
            for h in hyps:
                lines.append(f"  [{h['ts'][:19]}] {h['pair']}: {h['variable']} {h['old_value']} → {h['new_value']}")
                if h["reasoning"]:
                    lines.append(f"    Reasoning: {h['reasoning']}")
        lines.append("")

        # 8. TODAY section
        today_rows = conn.execute(
            "SELECT * FROM trades WHERE bot=? AND (entry_ts LIKE ? OR exit_ts LIKE ?) ORDER BY COALESCE(exit_ts, entry_ts) ASC",
            (bot, f"{today_str}%", f"{today_str}%"),
        ).fetchall()
        today_closed = [r for r in today_rows if r["exit_reason"]]
        t_pnls = [r["pnl_pct"] for r in today_closed if r["pnl_pct"] is not None]
        t_wins = [p for p in t_pnls if p > 0]
        today_total = sum(t_pnls) if t_pnls else 0
        today_wr = len(t_wins) / len(today_closed) * 100 if today_closed else 0

        lines.append("─" * 40)
        lines.append(f"TODAY ({today_str})")
        lines.append("─" * 40)
        lines.append(f"  Closed: {len(today_closed)} | PnL: {today_total:+.4f}% | WR: {today_wr:.1f}%")
        # Per-pair today breakdown
        today_by_pair = {}
        for r in today_closed:
            p = r["pair"] or "unknown"
            today_by_pair.setdefault(p, {"trades": 0, "pnl": 0, "wins": 0})
            today_by_pair[p]["trades"] += 1
            today_by_pair[p]["pnl"] += r["pnl_pct"] or 0
            if (r["pnl_pct"] or 0) > 0:
                today_by_pair[p]["wins"] += 1
        if today_by_pair:
            for p, d in sorted(today_by_pair.items()):
                wrp = d["wins"] / d["trades"] * 100 if d["trades"] else 0
                lines.append(f"    {p}: {d['trades']} trades | {d['pnl']:+.4f}% | {wrp:.1f}% WR")
        lines.append("")
        lines.append("=" * 50)
        lines.append("")

    conn.close()
    return "\n".join(lines)


def _first_trade_date(conn):
    row = conn.execute("SELECT entry_ts FROM trades ORDER BY entry_ts ASC LIMIT 1").fetchone()
    if row and row["entry_ts"]:
        return row["entry_ts"][:10]
    return "—"


def _fetch_live_indicators(pairs):
    """Compute RSI, ADX, BB for each pair from yfinance. Uses pure Python (no numpy)."""
    import warnings
    warnings.filterwarnings("ignore")
    import yfinance as yf

    def _rsi(prices, period=14):
        if len(prices) < period + 1: return None
        chg = prices[-1] - prices[-period-1]
        gains = sum(max(prices[i] - prices[i-1], 0) for i in range(-period, 0))
        losses = sum(max(prices[i-1] - prices[i], 0) for i in range(-period, 0))
        if losses == 0: return 100.0
        rs = gains / losses if losses else 0
        return round(100.0 - (100.0 / (1.0 + rs)), 1)

    def _adx(prices, period=14):
        if len(prices) < period * 2: return None
        up_sum = sum(max(prices[i] - prices[i-1], 0) for i in range(-period, 0))
        down_sum = sum(max(prices[i-1] - prices[i], 0) for i in range(-period, 0))
        tr_sum = sum(max(prices[i] - prices[i-1], 0) for i in range(-period, 0))
        atr = tr_sum / period if period else 0
        if atr == 0: return None
        plus_di = 100 * up_sum / period / atr
        minus_di = 100 * down_sum / period / atr
        dx = 100 * abs(plus_di - minus_di) / (plus_di + minus_di) if (plus_di + minus_di) > 0 else 0
        return round(dx, 1)

    def _bb(prices, period=20, std_dev=1.5):
        if len(prices) < period: return None, None, None, None
        recent = prices[-period:]
        ma = sum(recent) / period
        variance = sum((x - ma) ** 2 for x in recent) / period
        sd = variance ** 0.5
        lower = round(ma - std_dev * sd, 5)
        upper = round(ma + std_dev * sd, 5)
        bw = round(2 * std_dev * sd / ma * 100, 4) if ma > 0 else 0
        return round(ma, 5), upper, lower, bw

    result = {}
    for pair in pairs:
        ticker = PAIR_TICKERS.get(pair)
        if not ticker:
            result[pair] = {}
            continue
        try:
            df = yf.download(ticker, period="2d", interval="5m", progress=False, auto_adjust=True)
            if df is None or len(df) < 50:
                result[pair] = {}
                continue
            if hasattr(df.columns, "levels"):
                df.columns = df.columns.get_level_values(0)
            df = df.dropna(subset=["Close"]).copy()
            closes = [float(v) for v in df["Close"].values]
            price = round(closes[-1], 5)
            rsi = _rsi(closes)
            adx = _adx(closes)
            bm, bu, bl, bw = _bb(closes)

            bb_pos = "?"
            if bm and bl and bw is not None:
                if price <= bl * 1.001:
                    bb_pos = "touching lower"
                elif price <= (bl + bm) / 2:
                    bb_pos = "near lower"
                elif price >= bm * 0.999:
                    bb_pos = "at middle"
                elif price >= bu * 0.999:
                    bb_pos = "at upper"
                else:
                    bb_pos = "above middle"

            result[pair] = {
                "rsi": rsi if rsi is not None else "?",
                "adx": adx if adx is not None else "?",
                "price": price,
                "bb_position": bb_pos,
                "bb_lower": bl,
                "bb_mid": bm,
                "bb_upper": bu,
                "bb_bandwidth": bw,
            }
        except Exception as e:
            result[pair] = {"error": str(e)[:80]}
    return result


PAIR_MAP = {
    "EUR_USD": "EUR/USD", "GBP_USD": "GBP/USD",
    "AUD_USD": "AUD/USD", "GBP_JPY": "GBP/JPY",
    "XAU_USD": "XAU/USD", "XAG_USD": "XAG/USD",
}
SLASH_PAIRS = {v: k for k, v in PAIR_MAP.items()}

@app.get("/api/export-text/pair/{pair_name}", response_class=PlainTextResponse)
def export_pair(pair_name: str):
    pair = PAIR_MAP.get(pair_name, pair_name.replace("_", "/"))
    conn = get_conn()

    # All trades for this pair
    bot = "forex" if pair.count("/") == 1 and pair not in ("XAU/USD", "XAG/USD") else "gold"
    trades = conn.execute("SELECT * FROM trades WHERE bot=? AND pair=? ORDER BY COALESCE(exit_ts, entry_ts) ASC", (bot, pair)).fetchall()
    closed = [r for r in trades if r["exit_reason"]]
    pnls = [r["pnl_pct"] for r in closed if r["pnl_pct"] is not None]
    wins = [p for p in pnls if p > 0]
    losses = [p for p in pnls if p <= 0]

    # State + strategy
    state_row = conn.execute("SELECT * FROM latest_state WHERE bot=?", (bot,)).fetchone()
    strat_all = json.loads(state_row["strategy_json"]) if state_row and state_row["strategy_json"] else {}
    hb = json.loads(state_row["heartbeat_json"]) if state_row and state_row["heartbeat_json"] else {}
    open_json = json.loads(state_row["open_trades_json"]) if state_row and state_row["open_trades_json"] else []
    s = strat_all.get(pair, {})
    entry_conf = s.get("entry", {})

    # Hypotheses + skips
    hyps = conn.execute("SELECT * FROM hypotheses WHERE bot=? AND pair=? ORDER BY ts DESC", (bot, pair)).fetchall()
    skips = conn.execute("SELECT * FROM skips WHERE bot=? AND pair=? ORDER BY ts DESC", (bot, pair)).fetchall()
    first_trade = trades[0]["entry_ts"][:10] if trades else "—"
    now_str = utcnow_iso()[:19]
    conn.close()

    # Current open trade
    open_trade = None
    for t in open_json:
        if t.get("asset") == pair:
            open_trade = t
            break

    # Calculate stats
    total_pnl = sum(pnls) if pnls else 0
    avg_win = sum(wins) / len(wins) if wins else 0
    avg_loss = sum(losses) / len(losses) if losses else 0
    pf = abs(avg_win / avg_loss) if avg_loss != 0 else 0
    avg_hold = (sum([r["hold_cycles"] for r in closed if r["hold_cycles"]]) / len([r for r in closed if r["hold_cycles"]])) if closed and any(r["hold_cycles"] for r in closed) else 0
    max_dd = min(pnls) if pnls else 0
    pairs_pnls = [r["pnl_pct"] for r in closed if r["pnl_pct"] is not None]

    # Exit breakdown
    stops = sum(1 for r in closed if r["exit_reason"] == "stop_loss")
    targets = sum(1 for r in closed if r["exit_reason"] == "profit_target")
    timeouts = sum(1 for r in closed if r["exit_reason"] == "time_exit")

    lines = []
    # Header
    lines.append("=" * 50)
    lines.append(f"HERMES — {pair} PAIR ANALYSIS")
    lines.append(f"Generated: {now_str}")
    stype = s.get("strategy_type", "?")
    ver = s.get("version", "?")
    lines.append(f"Strategy: {stype} | Version: {ver}")
    lines.append("=" * 50)
    lines.append("")

    # CURRENT STATE
    lines.append("## CURRENT STATE")
    regimes = hb.get("regimes", {})
    live = hb.get("live_indicators", {}) or {}
    chart_ctxs = hb.get("chart_contexts", {}) or {}
    lv = live.get(pair, {})
    rsi = lv.get("rsi", "?")
    adx = lv.get("adx", "?")
    regime = regimes.get(pair, "?")
    bb_pos = lv.get("bb_position", "?")
    bb_lower = lv.get("bb_lower", "?")
    bb_mid = lv.get("bb_mid", "?")
    bb_upper = lv.get("bb_upper", "?")
    bb_bw = lv.get("bb_bandwidth", "?")
    bb_std = s.get("bb_std_dev", "1.5")
    chart_txt = chart_ctxs.get(pair, "") or lv.get("chart_context", "")
    sess = entry_conf.get("session_filter", "24h")
    in_sess = "yes" if sess == "24h" else "?"

    thresh = entry_conf.get("mr_entry_rsi") or entry_conf.get("threshold", "?")
    adx_th = s.get("adx_threshold", "?")
    bear_ok = s.get("allow_bear_entries", False)
    last_price = hb.get("last_price", "?")
    last_cycle = hb.get("ts", "?")[:19] if hb.get("ts") else "?"
    lines.append(f"  RSI: {rsi} | Threshold: {thresh}")
    lines.append(f"  ADX: {adx} | Threshold: {adx_th}")
    lines.append(f"  Regime: {regime} | Bear entries: {bear_ok}")
    lines.append(f"  BB Position: {bb_pos} | Lower={bb_lower} Mid={bb_mid} Upper={bb_upper}")
    lines.append(f"  BB Std Dev: {bb_std} | Bandwidth: {bb_bw}")
    lines.append(f"  Chart context: {chart_txt}")
    lines.append(f"  Session filter: {sess} | In session: {in_sess}")
    lines.append(f"  Last price: {last_price} | Last update: {last_cycle}")
    lines.append("")

    # OPEN POSITION
    lines.append("## OPEN POSITION")
    if open_trade:
        entry_px = open_trade.get("entry_price", "?")
        entry_ts = open_trade.get("entry_ts", "?")
        held = open_trade.get("hold_cycles", "?")
        ur = open_trade.get("_unrealised_pct", 0)
        stop_px = open_trade.get("_stop_price", 0)
        target_px = entry_px * 1.03 if isinstance(entry_px, (int, float)) else 0
        stop_dist = ((stop_px - entry_px) / entry_px * 100) if isinstance(entry_px, (int, float)) and entry_px else 0
        tgt_dist = ((target_px - entry_px) / entry_px * 100) if isinstance(entry_px, (int, float)) and entry_px else 0
        held_mins = f"{held}c ({held} min)" if held != "?" else "?"
        entry_rsi = open_trade.get("_entry_rsi", "?")
        entry_reg = open_trade.get("_entry_regime", "?")
        entry_q = open_trade.get("entry_quality_score", "?")
        entry_ver = open_trade.get("strategy_version", "?")
        lines.append(f"  Status: OPEN")
        lines.append(f"  Entry price: {entry_px}")
        lines.append(f"  Entry time: {entry_ts}")
        lines.append(f"  Hold time: {held_mins}")
        lines.append(f"  Unrealised PnL: {ur:+.4f}%")
        lines.append(f"  Stop price: {stop_px} | Distance to stop: {stop_dist:+.3f}%")
        lines.append(f"  Target price: {target_px:.5f} | Distance to target: {tgt_dist:+.3f}%")
        lines.append(f"  Entry RSI: {entry_rsi}")
        lines.append(f"  Entry regime: {entry_reg}")
        lines.append(f"  Entry quality score: {entry_q}/10")
        lines.append(f"  Strategy version at entry: {entry_ver}")
    else:
        lines.append("  Status: none")
    lines.append("")

    # LIFETIME PERFORMANCE
    lines.append("## LIFETIME PERFORMANCE")
    lines.append(f"  Total closed trades: {len(closed)}")
    lines.append(f"  Win rate: {len(wins)/len(closed)*100:.1f}%" if closed else "  Win rate: N/A")
    lines.append(f"  Total PnL: {total_pnl:+.4f}%")
    lines.append(f"  Avg win: +{avg_win:.4f}%")
    lines.append(f"  Avg loss: {avg_loss:.4f}%")
    lines.append(f"  Profit factor: {pf:.3f}x")
    lines.append(f"  Avg hold time: {avg_hold:.0f} cycles")
    lines.append(f"  Max drawdown: {max_dd:+.4f}%")
    lines.append(f"  Tracking since: {first_trade}")
    lines.append("")
    tot = len(closed)
    lines.append(f"  Exit breakdown:")
    lines.append(f"    Stop loss:     {stops:>4} trades ({stops/tot*100:.0f}%)" if tot else f"    Stop loss:     {stops:>4}")
    lines.append(f"    Profit target: {targets:>4} trades ({targets/tot*100:.0f}%)" if tot else f"    Profit target: {targets:>4}")
    lines.append(f"    Time exit:     {timeouts:>4} trades ({timeouts/tot*100:.0f}%)" if tot else f"    Time exit:     {timeouts:>4}")
    lines.append("")

    # RECENT vs LIFETIME COMPARISON
    lines.append("## RECENT vs LIFETIME COMPARISON")
    windows = {}
    for label, n in [("Last 5", 5), ("Last 10", 10), ("Last 20", 20), ("Lifetime", len(closed))]:
        subset = closed[-n:]
        sub_pnls = [r["pnl_pct"] for r in subset if r["pnl_pct"] is not None]
        sub_wins = [p for p in sub_pnls if p > 0]
        sub_wr = len(sub_wins) / len(subset) * 100 if subset else 0
        sub_avg = sum(sub_pnls) / len(sub_pnls) if sub_pnls else 0
        windows[label] = (sub_wr, sub_avg)
    lines.append(f"                 {'Last 5':>10} {'Last 10':>10} {'Last 20':>10} {'Lifetime':>10}")
    wr5, a5 = windows.get("Last 5", (0, 0))
    wr10, a10 = windows.get("Last 10", (0, 0))
    wr20, a20 = windows.get("Last 20", (0, 0))
    wrL, aL = windows.get("Lifetime", (0, 0))
    lines.append(f"  Win rate:      {wr5:>9.1f}% {wr10:>9.1f}% {wr20:>9.1f}% {wrL:>9.1f}%")
    lines.append(f"  Avg PnL/trade: {a5:>9.4f}% {a10:>9.4f}% {a20:>9.4f}% {aL:>9.4f}%")
    trend = "improving" if wr5 > wrL + 10 else ("declining" if wr5 < wrL - 10 else "stable")
    lines.append(f"  Trend:         {trend:>10}")
    lines.append("")

    # LAST 10 TRADES
    lines.append("## LAST 10 TRADES (most recent first)")
    lines.append(f"  # | {'Date':<12} | {'Entry':<10} | {'Exit':<10} | {'PnL%':<10} | {'Exit reason':<15} | {'Hold':<6} | {'RSI':<6} | {'Regime':<8}")
    lines.append(f"  {'-'*3} {'-'*12} {'-'*10} {'-'*10} {'-'*10} {'-'*15} {'-'*6} {'-'*6} {'-'*8}")
    for idx, r in enumerate(reversed(closed[-10:]), 1):
        d = (r["entry_ts"] or "?")[:10]
        en = r["entry_price"] or "?"
        ex = r["exit_price"] or "?"
        pnl = r["pnl_pct"] or 0
        reason = r["exit_reason"] or "?"
        held = r["hold_cycles"] or "?"
        raw = json.loads(r["raw_json"]) if r["raw_json"] else {}
        ersi = raw.get("entry_rsi", "?")
        ereg = raw.get("entry_regime", "?")
        lines.append(f"  {idx:<3} | {d:<12} | {str(en):<10} | {str(ex):<10} | {pnl:<+9.4f}% | {reason:<15} | {str(held):<6} | {str(ersi):<6} | {ereg:<8}")
    lines.append("")

    # STRATEGY PARAMETERS
    lines.append("## STRATEGY PARAMETERS (current)")
    flat_params = {
        "strategy_type": s.get("strategy_type"),
        "version": s.get("version"),
        "mr_entry_rsi": entry_conf.get("mr_entry_rsi"),
        "rsi threshold": entry_conf.get("threshold"),
        "bb_std_dev": s.get("bb_std_dev"),
        "stop_loss_pct": s.get("stop_loss_pct"),
        "profit_target_pct": s.get("profit_target_pct"),
        "trailing_stop_pct": s.get("trailing_stop_pct"),
        "time_exit_cycles": s.get("time_exit_cycles"),
        "atr_multiplier": s.get("atr_multiplier"),
        "use_atr_floor": s.get("use_atr_floor"),
        "atr_floor_pct": s.get("atr_floor_pct"),
        "allow_bear_entries": s.get("allow_bear_entries"),
        "adx_threshold": s.get("adx_threshold"),
        "vol_threshold_pct": s.get("vol_threshold_pct"),
        "session_filter": entry_conf.get("session_filter"),
        "position_size_r": s.get("position_size_r"),
    }
    for k, v in flat_params.items():
        if v is not None:
            lines.append(f"  {k}: {v}")
    lines.append("")

    # REFLECTION HISTORY
    lines.append("## REFLECTION HISTORY (all for this pair)")
    if hyps:
        for h in hyps:
            ts = h["ts"][:16] if h["ts"] else "?"
            var = h["variable"]
            ov = h["old_value"]
            nv = h["new_value"]
            lines.append(f"  [{ts}] {var} {ov}→{nv}")
            if h["reasoning"]:
                lines.append(f"    Reasoning: {h['reasoning']}")
    else:
        lines.append("  No reflections for this pair.")
    lines.append("")

    # SKIP ANALYSIS
    lines.append("## SKIP ANALYSIS (last 20 skips)")
    skip_reasons = {}
    for sk in skips[:20]:
        r = sk["reason_skipped"]
        skip_reasons[r] = skip_reasons.get(r, 0) + 1
    total_skips = sum(skip_reasons.values())
    lines.append("  Most common skip reasons:")
    if skip_reasons:
        for r, c in sorted(skip_reasons.items(), key=lambda x: -x[1]):
            pct = c / total_skips * 100
            lines.append(f"    {r:<25}: {c:>3} times ({pct:.0f}%)")
    lines.append("")
    lines.append("  Recent skips (last 5):")
    for sk in skips[:5]:
        ts = sk["ts"][:16] if sk["ts"] else "?"
        rsn = sk["reason_skipped"]
        rsi_val = sk["rsi_at_skip"] or "?"
        px = sk["price_at_skip"] or "?"
        lines.append(f"    [{ts}] {rsn} | RSI: {rsi_val} | Price: {px}")
    lines.append("")

    # WHAT WOULD TRIGGER AN ENTRY
    lines.append("## WHAT WOULD TRIGGER AN ENTRY RIGHT NOW")
    rsi_ok = isinstance(rsi, (int, float)) and isinstance(thresh, (int, float)) and rsi < thresh
    bb_ok = False
    try:
        lv_price = float(last_price) if last_price != "?" else 0
        lv_lower = float(bb_lower) if bb_lower != "?" else 0
        bb_ok = lv_price > 0 and lv_lower > 0 and lv_price <= lv_lower
    except (ValueError, TypeError):
        pass
    adx_ok = isinstance(adx, (int, float)) and isinstance(adx_th, (int, float)) and adx >= adx_th
    bear_blocked = regime == "BEAR" and not bear_ok

    conditions_met = sum([rsi_ok, bb_ok, adx_ok, not bear_blocked])
    if conditions_met == 4:
        lines.append("  READY TO ENTRY — all conditions met:")
        lines.append(f"    ✅ RSI {rsi} < threshold {thresh}")
        lines.append(f"    ✅ BB lower touch confirmed")
        lines.append(f"    ✅ ADX {adx} >= {adx_th}")
        lines.append(f"    ✅ Regime: {regime} (bear entries {'allowed' if bear_ok else 'blocked'})")
        lines.append("    → Entry would fire on next cycle")
    elif conditions_met >= 2:
        lines.append(f"  CLOSE TO ENTRY — {conditions_met} conditions met, {4 - conditions_met} blocking:")
        lines.append(f"    {'✅' if rsi_ok else '❌'} RSI {rsi} {'<' if rsi_ok else '>='} threshold {thresh}" + (f" (gap {rsi - thresh:.1f} pts)" if not rsi_ok else ""))
        lines.append(f"    {'✅' if bb_ok else '❌'} BB lower{' not yet ' if not bb_ok else ' '}touched" + (f" (price {last_price}, BB lower {bb_lower})" if not bb_ok else ""))
        lines.append(f"    {'✅' if adx_ok else '❌'} ADX {adx} {'>=' if adx_ok else '<'} {adx_th}" + (f" (gap {adx_th - adx:.1f})" if not adx_ok else ""))
        if regime == "BEAR":
            lines.append(f"    {'⚠' if bear_ok else '❌'} Bear entries: {'allowed' if bear_ok else 'blocked'}")
        lines.append(f"    → Nearest trigger: {'RSI drop to ' + str(thresh) if not rsi_ok else 'price touch BB lower' if not bb_ok else 'ADX rising to ' + str(adx_th)}")
    else:
        lines.append("  NOT CLOSE — blocked by:")
        lines.append(f"    ❌ RSI {rsi} >= threshold {thresh}" if not rsi_ok else "")
        lines.append(f"    ❌ BB lower not touched" if not bb_ok else "")
        lines.append(f"    ❌ ADX {adx} < {adx_th}" if not adx_ok else "")
        if not bear_ok and regime == "BEAR":
            lines.append(f"    ❌ Bear regime — bear entries blocked")
        lines.append(f"    → Furthest from entry on: RSI" if not rsi_ok else "BB touch" if not bb_ok else "ADX" if not adx_ok else "regime")

    lines.append("")
    lines.append("=" * 50)
    return "\n".join(lines)


# ───────────────────── Auth ─────────────────────


@app.get("/api/auth/status")
def auth_status():
    conn = get_conn()
    row = conn.execute("SELECT value FROM app_config WHERE key='dashboard_password'").fetchone()
    conn.close()
    return {"setup_required": row is None}


@app.post("/api/auth/setup")
async def auth_setup(request: Request):
    conn = get_conn()
    row = conn.execute("SELECT value FROM app_config WHERE key='dashboard_password'").fetchone()
    if row:
        conn.close()
        raise HTTPException(400, "Already configured")

    body = await request.json()
    password = body.get("password", "")
    confirm = body.get("confirm", "")

    if password != confirm:
        conn.close()
        raise HTTPException(400, "Passwords do not match")
    if len(password) < 6:
        conn.close()
        raise HTTPException(400, "Password too short")

    # Save password locally
    conn.execute("INSERT OR REPLACE INTO app_config (key, value) VALUES ('dashboard_password', ?)", (password,))
    conn.commit()

    # Generate auth token
    token = secrets.token_hex(32)
    now = utcnow_iso()
    expires = (datetime.now(timezone.utc) + timedelta(days=7)).isoformat()
    conn.execute("INSERT INTO auth_tokens (token, created_at, expires_at) VALUES (?, ?, ?)",
                 (token, now, expires))
    conn.commit()
    conn.close()

    return {"token": token, "message": "Password set"}


@app.post("/api/auth/login")
async def auth_login(request: Request):
    conn = get_conn()
    row = conn.execute("SELECT value FROM app_config WHERE key='dashboard_password'").fetchone()
    if not row:
        conn.close()
        raise HTTPException(400, "Setup required first")

    body = await request.json()
    password = body.get("password", "")

    if password != row["value"]:
        conn.close()
        raise HTTPException(401, "Incorrect password")

    token = secrets.token_hex(32)
    now = utcnow_iso()
    expires = (datetime.now(timezone.utc) + timedelta(days=7)).isoformat()
    conn.execute("INSERT INTO auth_tokens (token, created_at, expires_at) VALUES (?, ?, ?)",
                 (token, now, expires))
    conn.commit()
    conn.close()

    return {"token": token}


@app.get("/api/auth/verify")
def auth_verify(request: Request):
    auth_header = request.headers.get("Authorization", "")
    if not auth_header.startswith("Bearer "):
        return {"valid": False}

    token = auth_header[7:]
    now = utcnow_iso()

    conn = get_conn()
    # Cleanup expired tokens
    conn.execute("DELETE FROM auth_tokens WHERE expires_at < ?", (now,))
    conn.commit()

    row = conn.execute(
        "SELECT * FROM auth_tokens WHERE token=? AND expires_at > ?",
        (token, now),
    ).fetchone()
    conn.close()

    return {"valid": row is not None}


class ChatBody(BaseModel):
    question: str


@app.post("/api/chat")
def chat(body: ChatBody):
    """Answer a question about the bot's trading activity using available data."""
    question = body.question.strip().lower()
    if not question:
        raise HTTPException(400, "question is required")

    conn = get_conn()
    forex_state = conn.execute("SELECT * FROM latest_state WHERE bot='forex'").fetchone()
    forex_trades = conn.execute("SELECT * FROM trades WHERE bot='forex' ORDER BY COALESCE(exit_ts, entry_ts) DESC LIMIT 10").fetchall()
    forex_strat = json.loads(forex_state["strategy_json"]) if forex_state and forex_state["strategy_json"] else {}
    forex_heartbeat = json.loads(forex_state["heartbeat_json"]) if forex_state and forex_state["heartbeat_json"] else {}
    forex_open = json.loads(forex_state["open_trades_json"]) if forex_state and forex_state["open_trades_json"] else []
    conn.close()

    open_summary = [f"{t.get('asset','?')}: entry {t.get('entry_price','?')}, PnL {t.get('_unrealised_pct',0):+.4f}%, stop {t.get('_stop_price','?')}, held {t.get('hold_cycles','?')}c" for t in forex_open]
    recent = [f"{t['pair']}: {t['pnl_pct']:+.4f}% ({t['exit_reason']})" for t in forex_trades[:5]]
    regimes = forex_heartbeat.get("regimes", {})
    cycle = forex_heartbeat.get("cycle", "?")

    lines = []
    if "trade" in question and ("how long" in question or "duration" in question or "estimate" in question):
        if open_summary:
            lines.append("Currently open:")
            lines.extend(f"  • {s}" for s in open_summary)
            lines.append("Mean reversion trades typically exit within 1-6 hours when price bounces to the BB middle band.")
        else:
            lines.append("No open trades. Bot is waiting for entry conditions.")
            lines.append(f"Regimes: {regimes}")
    elif any(w in question for w in ["win rate", "performance", "profit", "how many"]):
        wins = sum(1 for t in forex_trades if t.get("pnl_pct", 0) > 0)
        total = len(forex_trades)
        lines.append(f"Last {total} trades: {wins}/{total} wins ({wins/total*100:.0f}%)" if total else "No trades yet.")
        lines.extend(f"  {s}" for s in recent)
    elif "strategy" in question or "config" in question:
        for pair, s in forex_strat.items():
            if isinstance(s, dict):
                e = s.get("entry", {})
                lines.append(f"  {pair}: {s.get('strategy_type')}, RSI<{e.get('mr_entry_rsi') or e.get('threshold','?')}, stop {s.get('stop_loss_pct','?')}%, floor {s.get('atr_floor_pct','none')}")
    else:
        lines.append(f"Cycle {cycle}. {'No open trades.' if not open_summary else f'{len(forex_open)} open trade(s).'}")
        lines.append(f"Regimes: {regimes}")

    return {"answer": "\n".join(lines), "question": question}


@app.get("/api/per-version/{bot_name}")
def per_version_performance(bot_name: str, pair: Optional[str] = None):
    if bot_name not in VALID_BOTS:
        raise HTTPException(404, f"Unknown bot '{bot_name}'")

    # Only process trades for pairs that currently exist for this bot
    valid_pairs = set(PAIR_TICKERS.keys())
    if bot_name == "forex":
        valid_pairs = set(FOREX_PAIRS)
    elif bot_name == "gold":
        valid_pairs = {"XAU/USD", "XAG/USD"}

    conn = get_conn()
    query = "SELECT raw_json, pnl_pct, exit_reason, pair FROM trades WHERE bot=? AND exit_reason IS NOT NULL"
    params = [bot_name]
    if pair:
        query += " AND pair=?"
        params.append(pair)
    rows = conn.execute(query, params).fetchall()
    conn.close()

    versions = {}
    pair_versions = {}
    for r in rows:
        p = r["pair"] or "?"
        # Skip stale pairs (BTC/USDT, ETH/USDT, SOL/USDT, etc.)
        if p not in valid_pairs:
            continue

        raw = json.loads(r["raw_json"]) if r["raw_json"] else {}
        v = raw.get("strategy_version") or str(r.get("strategy_type", "") or "")
        if not v or v == "None":
            v = "?"
        pnl = r["pnl_pct"] if r["pnl_pct"] else 0

        # Per-version aggregate
        if v not in versions:
            versions[v] = {"trades": 0, "pnls": [], "wins": 0, "stops": 0, "targets": 0, "timeouts": 0, "pairs": set()}
        versions[v]["trades"] += 1
        versions[v]["pnls"].append(pnl)
        if pnl > 0:
            versions[v]["wins"] += 1
        if r["exit_reason"] == "stop_loss":
            versions[v]["stops"] += 1
        elif r["exit_reason"] == "profit_target":
            versions[v]["targets"] += 1
        elif r["exit_reason"] == "time_exit":
            versions[v]["timeouts"] += 1
        versions[v]["pairs"].add(p)

        # Per-pair within version
        pkey = f"{v}::{p}"
        if pkey not in pair_versions:
            pair_versions[pkey] = {"pair": p, "trades": 0, "pnls": [], "wins": 0, "stops": 0, "targets": 0, "timeouts": 0}
        pair_versions[pkey]["trades"] += 1
        pair_versions[pkey]["pnls"].append(pnl)
        if pnl > 0:
            pair_versions[pkey]["wins"] += 1
        if r["exit_reason"] == "stop_loss":
            pair_versions[pkey]["stops"] += 1
        elif r["exit_reason"] == "profit_target":
            pair_versions[pkey]["targets"] += 1
        elif r["exit_reason"] == "time_exit":
            pair_versions[pkey]["timeouts"] += 1

    result = []
    sorted_v = sorted(versions.keys())
    for i, v in enumerate(sorted_v):
        vs = versions[v]
        avg = sum(vs["pnls"]) / len(vs["pnls"]) if vs["pnls"] else 0
        wr = vs["wins"] / len(vs["pnls"]) * 100 if vs["pnls"] else 0
        total = sum(vs["pnls"])
        change = ""
        if i > 0:
            prev_v = sorted_v[i - 1]
            prev_avg = sum(versions[prev_v]["pnls"]) / len(versions[prev_v]["pnls"]) if versions[prev_v]["pnls"] else 0
            if avg > prev_avg:
                change = "improved"
            elif avg < prev_avg:
                change = "declined"

        # Build per-pair breakdown for this version
        pair_rows = []
        for pkey, pv in sorted(pair_versions.items()):
            if pkey.startswith(f"{v}::"):
                p_avg = sum(pv["pnls"]) / len(pv["pnls"]) if pv["pnls"] else 0
                p_wr = pv["wins"] / len(pv["pnls"]) * 100 if pv["pnls"] else 0
                p_total = sum(pv["pnls"])
                pair_rows.append({
                    "pair": pv["pair"],
                    "trades": pv["trades"],
                    "win_rate": round(p_wr, 1),
                    "avg_pnl": round(p_avg, 4),
                    "total_pnl": round(p_total, 4),
                    "stops": pv["stops"],
                    "targets": pv["targets"],
                    "timeouts": pv["timeouts"],
                })

        result.append({
            "version": v, "trades": vs["trades"], "win_rate": round(wr, 1),
            "avg_pnl": round(avg, 4), "total_pnl": round(total, 4),
            "stops": vs["stops"], "targets": vs["targets"], "timeouts": vs["timeouts"],
            "pairs": sorted(vs["pairs"]), "trend": change,
            "pair_breakdown": pair_rows,
        })
    return {"bot": bot_name, "versions": result}


if __name__ == "__main__":
    import uvicorn
    uvicorn.run(app, host="0.0.0.0", port=int(os.getenv("PORT", 8000)))
```



═══════════════════════════════════════════════════

### hermes-crypto/state/strategies/BTC_USD.yaml  (29 lines)
```yaml
version: '01'
strategy_type: mean_reversion
entry:
  indicator: bollinger_band
  direction: long
  mr_entry_rsi: 40
  bb_std_dev: 1.5
  session_filter: 24h
stop_loss_pct: 2.5
profit_target_pct: 5.0
trailing_stop_pct: 0.5
time_exit_cycles: 720
position_size_r: 0.20
atr_multiplier: 2.0
minimum_entry_quality: 0
adx_threshold: 15
vol_threshold_pct: 0.50
vol_max_pct: 2.0
vol_min_pct: 0.20
use_atr_floor: true
atr_floor_pct: 0.8
allow_bear_entries: false
# v01: Mean reversion on Bitcoin (BTC/USD) — crypto-adapted
# Entry: BB lower touch + RSI < 40 + ADX >= 15 (24/7 market, no session block)
# Stop: 2.5% (wider for crypto vol — ATR typically 1-5% vs forex 0.05-0.15%)
# Target: 5.0% (2:1 RR)
# Position: 0.20R (half-risk until 25 trades)
# ATR floor: 0.8% prevents absurdly tight stops during vol spikes
# Note: Crypto trends more than mean-reverts — monitor for strategy_type swap
```

APPENDIX B — GP ENGINE COMPLETE SPECIFICATION
═══════════════════════════════════════════════════

> All values below are extracted VERBATIM from genetic_discovery.py, gp_intelligence.py,
> backtest.py, hermes_gp_cron.py. Variable names match the actual source.

─────────────────────────────────────────────
B.1 EXPRESSION TREE GRAMMAR
─────────────────────────────────────────────
A GP individual is a nested list: [OP, CHILD1, CHILD2, ...] or a TERMINAL (string/number).
Terminals are resolved to a 1-D signal series of the same length as `prices`.

TERMINALS (15, verified in genetic_discovery.py — macro series fetched in _fetch_macro):
  "close"      -> close price series
  "open"       -> open price series
  "high"       -> high price series
  "low"        -> low price series
  "volume"     -> volume series
  "rsi"        -> RSI(14) series
  "atr"        -> ATR(14) series
  "adx"        -> ADX(14) series
  "bb_upper"    -> Bollinger upper(20,2) series
  "bb_lower"    -> Bollinger lower(20,2) series
  "sma_50"     -> SMA(50) series
  "sma_200"    -> SMA(200) series
  "returns"     -> pct-change of close series
  "dxy"        -> macro: US Dollar Index series
  "vix"        -> macro: volatility index series
  (additional macro fetched: TNX, SPX, oil, gold — see _fetch_macro)

OPERATORS (exact implementation in _eval_tree, backtest.py ~line 1240-1279):
  "+"   -> element-wise addition v1[i]+v2[i]
  "-"   -> element-wise subtraction
  "*"   -> element-wise multiplication
  "/"   -> element-wise division, guarded: divisor clipped to [-1e-9,1e-9]? -> 0.0
  "abs" -> abs(v1)
  "sqrt"-> math.sqrt(max(0, v1[i]))
  "log" -> math.log(max(1e-9, v1[i]))
  "exp" -> math.exp(min(50, v1[i]))
  "min" -> element-wise min(v1,v2)
  "max" -> element-wise max(v1,v2)
  "sma" -> simple moving average over window v2 (period = int(v2[-1]))
  "ema" -> EMA over window v2
  "stddev" -> rolling stddev over window v2
  "lag" -> lag series by int(v2[-1]) periods
  "highest" -> rolling max over window v2
  "lowest" -> rolling min over window v2
  "sma_diff"-> fast SMA - slow SMA (see source lines 1271-1276)
  primitive math ops ("+","-","*","/","abs","sqrt","log","exp","min","max", etc.)
    are applied element-wise via _PRIMITIVES dispatch (line 1277).

B.2 FITNESS FUNCTION (exact)
─────────────────────────────────────────────
In _compute_fitness (genetic_discovery.py):
  fitness = | Pearson(signal, next_candle_return) | - 0.001 * complexity
  where:
    signal            = the GP tree's output series, normalised (see _signal_to_trades)
    next_candle_return = pct change of close at i+1
    Pearson(a,b)      = covariance(a,b) / (std(a)*std(b))
    complexity         = _count_nodes(expr)  (number of nodes in the tree)
    penalty coeff     = 0.001  (hard-coded, line: `fitness = corr - 0.001 * complexity`)
  After evolution, survivors are re-tested on a 40% holdout set; only those with
  OOS Pearson correlation >= 0.15 are accepted (0.15 = 99th percentile of random,
  per CHANGELOG).

B.3 LIVE ENSEMBLE WEIGHTED VOTE FORMULA (gp_intelligence.py, variable names match)
─────────────────────────────────────────────
In evaluate/promote path (load_discovered_indicators + loop check_discovered_signals):
  For each LIVE indicator `ind` whose (_expr) signal at the current bar is computable:
      s = ind["signal_value"]          # current normalised signal for this indicator
      w = ind["fitness"]              # |OOS corr| stored at registration
      wr = get_indicator_live_wr(ind_name)   # running win rate of this indicator's signals
      total_weight += w * (wr if wr else 0.5)
      if s > 0.2:  bullish += w * (wr if wr else 0.5)
      elif s < -0.2: bearish += w * (wr if wr else 0.5)
  signal = (bullish - bearish) / max(total_weight, 0.001)
  n = number of indicators with |s| >= 0.2
  Labels (exact thresholds):
      signal > 0.5  and n/total >= 0.60  -> "strong_bullish"
      signal > 0.2  and n/total >= 0.50  -> "bullish"
      signal < -0.2 and n/total >= 0.50  -> "bearish"
      signal < -0.5 and n/total >= 0.60  -> "strong_bearish"
      else                                -> "conflict"
  ENTRY GATE (loop.py, verified L29):
      ensemble == "strong_bullish" and n >= 2 and rsi < 65 and weight >= 0.5
      and adx >= 20 and gp_bad_losses < 3 and gp_entry_score >= 0

B.4 SHADOW-TO-LIVE PROMOTION CONDITIONS (exact)
─────────────────────────────────────────────
register_discovered_indicator(expr, fitness, ...) writes to state/discovered/{PAIR}.json
with status="shadow" (NOT in the live voting pool yet — see _SHARED / load path).
  record_indicator_shadow / shadow_scorecard (backtest.py):
    - indicator starts in SHADOW mode; its signals are evaluated every bar but
      NEVER affect entries (L60: shadow-only until promoted).
    - promotion requires (verified minimums):
        * >= 50 shadow signals recorded (degradation tracker _live_deg.json counts)
        * shadow win_rate >= 40%   (same threshold as degradation cull, see B.6)
        * no crisis-window failure (crisis_validate WR >= 40% in each of 4 windows)
    - on promote: status flips to "live"; indicator enters the weighted-vote pool.
  NOTE: the exact numeric promotion minimums are enforced in hermes_gp_cron.py
  (phase0 WR>=45% & PnL>0 over 6mo; crisis WR>=40% over 4 windows;
  redundancy |r|>0.8 reject; walk-forward replace only if best beats prior by >10% WR
  AND better PnL). The per-cycle shadow→live promotion uses the 50-signal / 40%-WR rule.

B.5 FINGERPRINT SIMILARITY (exact)
─────────────────────────────────────────────
similar_gp_entries(pair, regime, adx, vol_pct, session, rsi, limit=5) (gp_intelligence.py:201):
  Builds query fingerprint fp = {pair, regime, adx(rounded 1dp), vol_pct(4dp), session, rsi(1dp)}.
  For each historical GP entry `e` with a known outcome:
      score = 0
      if e.pair == fp.pair:                       score += 5
      if e.regime == fp.regime:                   score += 3
      if abs(e.adx - fp.adx) < 5:                   score += 2
      if abs(e.vol_pct - fp.vol_pct) < 0.02:        score += 2
      if e.session == fp.session:                   score += 2
      if abs(e.rsi - fp.rsi) < 10:                  score += 1
  Return top-`limit` entries sorted by score desc.
  This score blends into gp_entry_score via:
      wr = wins / len(similar)
      score += (wr - 0.5) * 0.6        # ±0.3 contribution
  (gp_intelligence.py:272-276).

B.6 ALLOCATION MATRIX (exact)
─────────────────────────────────────────────
_condition_key(pair, regime, adx, vol_pct, session) (gp_intelligence.py:162):
      ab = "trending" if (adx or 0) >= 25 else "moderate" if (adx or 0) >= 20 else "ranging"
      vb = "high" if (vol_pct or 0) > 0.1 else "low"
      ref_pair = pair.split("/")[0]        # EUR, GBP, AUD, XAU, etc.
      key = f"{ref_pair}|{regime}|{ab}|{vb}|{session}"
  _update_matrix(key, entry_type, pnl_pct, stype) (line 169) on EVERY GP and traditional
  outcome writes into state/allocation_matrix.json:
      matrix[key]["gp"]       = {n, wins, pnl}
      matrix[key]["traditional"] = {n, wins, pnl}
  prefer_gp_over_traditional(ck, min_data=5) (line 178):
      if both gp.n >= 5 and trad.n >= 5:
          gp_wr = gp.wins/gp.n ; trad_wr = trad.wins/trad.n
          if gp_wr > trad_wr + 0.15: return True
          if trad_wr > gp_wr + 0.15: return False
      if gp.n >= 5: return gp.wins/gp.n >= 0.5
      return None   # insufficient data

B.7 EXILE vs DEGRADATION (exact numeric thresholds)
─────────────────────────────────────────────
DECISION_CORTEX (decision_cortex.py) — EXILE (permanent ban from GP voting):
      auto-exile when indicator GP-entry win rate < 30% after >= 5 GP-entry attempts.
      exile_decay: reconsider after 100 entries; reinstate if WR >= 40%.
GP_INTELLIGENCE degradation (backtest _live_deg.json / gp_intelligence):
      DEGRADE (not exile): indicator culled from live pool when live win_rate < 40%
      over >= 50 signals. (culled flag set in _live_deg.json.)
      Distinction: exile = cortex-level permanent ban (<30% WR, 5 attempts);
      degradation = gp_intelligence-level temporary removal (<40% WR, 50 signals).

B.8 CHICKEN-AND-EGG DEADLOCK (BUG TO FIX)
─────────────────────────────────────────────
gp_entry_score() (gp_intelligence.py:248) DEFAULTS to -0.3 (line 253):
      score = -0.3   # Default: slightly suppress — requires positive evidence to overcome
  The entry GATE demands gp_entry_score >= 0 (loop.py L29, gp_entry_score>=0).
  A brand-new pair has NO historical GP entries -> score stays -0.3 -> gate fails ->
  GP never takes its first entries -> never accumulates outcome data -> score never
  rises above 0. Deadlock.
  FIX (rebuild, Section 11 FIX 2): default gp_entry_score = 0.0 (neutral).
  The L30 GP-probe path partially mitigates (fires at 25% size when cortex empty,
  cycle%probe_int(50)==0, bullish, n>=2) but still requires bullish+n>=2.

═══════════════════════════════════════════════════

APPENDIX C — COMPLETE DATA FLOW WITH EXACT FIELD NAMES
═══════════════════════════════════════════════════

> Every field name below is the literal JSON key written/read in source. Types are Python.
> "missing/malformed" column = behaviour when the field is absent or wrong-typed.

C.1 heartbeat.json  (writer: loop.write_heartbeat, loop.py:597)
┌─────────────────────┬──────────┬───────────┬──────────┬──────────────────────────────┐
│ field               │ type     │ writer    │ reader   │ missing/malformed            │
├─────────────────────┼──────────┼───────────┼──────────┼──────────────────────────────┤
│ ts                  │ str(ISO)│ loop      │ (none)   │ none — generated each cycle  │
│ asset               │ str      │ loop      │ (none)   │ none                         │
│ cycle               │ int      │ loop      │ (none)   │ none                         │
│ consecutive_failures│ int      │ loop      │ (none)   │ none                         │
│ last_price          │ float    │ loop      │ (none)   │ none                         │
│ status              │ str      │ loop      │ (none)   │ "ok" if consec<5 else       │
│                     │          │           │          │ "circuit_open"               │
└─────────────────────┴──────────┴───────────┴──────────┴──────────────────────────────┘
NOTE: dashboard does NOT read heartbeat.json; it uses its own bot_status table. The
loop writes heartbeat.json to STATE_DIR/heartbeat.json (atomic). The heartbeat monitor
(hermes_heartbeat.py) polls /api/bot/{bot}/pulse, NOT this file.

C.2 trades.jsonl  (writer: loop on close, loop.py:1648; reader: loop.load_trades,
   reflect.load_trades, dashboard upsert_trades). One JSON object per line:
┌─────────────────────┬──────────┬───────────────────────────┬─────────────────────────┐
│ field               │ type     │ writer                    │ missing/malformed       │
├─────────────────────┼──────────┼───────────────────────────┼─────────────────────────┤
│ id                  │ str      │ trade["id"]              │ set by open; required   │
│ asset               │ str      │ loop asset               │ required               │
│ pair                │ str      │ loop asset               │ required               │
│ bot_id              │ str      │ "hermes-forex"         │ constant               │
│ entry_price         │ float    │ trade["entry_price"]    │ required               │
│ exit_price          │ float    │ current_price           │ required               │
│ entry_ts            │ str(ISO) │ trade["entry_ts"]        │ required               │
│ exit_ts             │ str(ISO) │ now.isoformat()         │ required               │
│ pnl_pct            │ float    │ round(unrealised,4)     │ required               │
│ exit_reason         │ str      │ one of: stop_loss,      │ required               │
│                     │          │ profit_target,            │                       │
│                     │          │ trailing_stop, time_exit │                       │
│ strategy_version    │ str      │ strategy["version"]      │ default "?"           │
│ mode                │ str      │ "paper"                 │ constant               │
│ hold_cycles         │ int      │ hold_cycles             │ required               │
│ price_high          │ float    │ trade._peak_price       │ defaults to exit_price │
│ price_low           │ float    │ trade._low_price        │ defaults to exit_price │
│ entry_rsi           │ float/str│ trade.entry_rsi        │ "N/A" if missing      │
│ entry_adx           │ float/str│ trade.entry_adx        │ "N/A" if missing      │
│ entry_regime         │ str      │ trade.entry_regime      │ "N/A" if missing      │
│ chart_context        │ str      │ trade.chart_context      │ "unavailable"         │
│ entry_quality_score │ float/str│ trade.entry_quality_score│ "N/A" if missing     │
│ strategy_type       │ str      │ "mean_reversion"/       │ required               │
│                     │          │ "rsi_momentum"/"gp_ensemble" │                  │
│ entry_session       │ str/int  │ _get_session(entry hour)│ 0 if unparseable     │
│ entry_day           │ str      │ _get_day_name(entry_ts) │ "UNKNOWN"             │
│ entry_hour_utc     │ int      │ hour of entry_ts         │ 0 if unparseable     │
│ concurrent_entries  │ int      │ len(_open_trades)       │ required               │
│ regime_changes      │ int      │ trade._regime_changes   │ 0                     │
│ exit_regime         │ str      │ best_fast_regime/        │ "UNKNOWN"             │
│                     │          │ best_regime              │                       │
│ market_move_pct     │ float    │ (exit-entry)/entry*100  │ required               │
│ alpha_pct           │ float    │ pnl - market_move       │ required               │
└─────────────────────┴──────────┴───────────────────────────┴─────────────────────────┘
Malformed line: load_trades does json.loads per line; a blank/partial line raises and
the whole loop call is wrapped in try/except Exception: pass (silent skip — see P7).

C.3 hypotheses.jsonl  (writer: reflect via decision_cortex.record_hypothesis /
   combined_reflect; reader: reflect.load_hypotheses). CURRENT STATE: empty.
┌─────────────────────┬──────────┬───────────────────────────┬─────────────────────────┐
│ field               │ type     │ notes                     │ missing/malformed       │
├─────────────────────┼──────────┼───────────────────────────┼─────────────────────────┤
│ ts                  │ str(ISO)│ timestamp                │ generated              │
│ pair                │ str      │ pair name                │ required               │
│ version_from        │ str      │ prior strategy version   │ required               │
│ version_to          │ str      │ proposed version        │ required               │
│ variable            │ str      │ param changed           │ required               │
│ old_value           │ str      │ previous value          │ required               │
│ new_value           │ str      │ proposed value          │ required               │
│ reasoning           │ str      │ LLM reasoning           │ optional               │
│ mode                │ str      │ "L1"/"L2"              │ required               │
│ scored              │ bool     │ True once scored        │ default False          │
│ score               │ int      │ >=0 if beneficial       │ 0 if unscored         │
└─────────────────────┴──────────┴───────────────────────────┴─────────────────────────┘
NOTE: the dashboard `hypotheses` SQLite table maps these (bot, ts, pair, version_from,
version_to, variable, old_value, new_value, reasoning, mode, raw_json) with PRIMARY KEY (bot, ts, variable).

C.4 strategy YAML  (writer: reflect.save_yaml / loop._seed_default_state;
   reader: loop.load_strategy_for_pair, reflect.load_yaml). Fields + valid ranges
   (from validate_strategy_params / STRATEGY_PARAM_RANGES in reflect.py):
┌─────────────────────┬──────────┬───────────────────────────┬─────────────────────────┐
│ field               │ type     │ valid range               │ default               │
├─────────────────────┼──────────┼───────────────────────────┼─────────────────────────┤
│ version             │ str      │ any (e.g. "07")         │ required               │
│ strategy_type       │ str      │ mean_reversion |         │ required (CORRECT:     │
│                     │          │ rsi_momentum            │ gold MUST be momentum) │
│ entry.indicator     │ str      │ bollinger_band | rsi     │ required               │
│ entry.direction     │ str      │ long                     │ required               │
│ entry.mr_entry_rsi │ float    │ ~30-45                   │ required (MR)         │
│ entry.threshold    │ float    │ ~30-60                   │ required (RSI/momentum)│
│ entry.bb_std_dev   │ float    │ ~1.0-3.0                │ 1.5/2.0             │
│ entry.session_filter│ str      │ london_only|ny_only|    │ required               │
│                     │          │ asian_only|24h          │                       │
│ stop_loss_pct      │ float    │ 0.5 – 10.0              │ 1.5                  │
│ profit_target_pct   │ float    │ 0.5 – 20.0              │ 3.0                  │
│ trailing_stop_pct   │ float    │ 0.0 – 5.0               │ 0.5                  │
│ time_exit_cycles    │ int      │ 60 – 1440               │ 360 (gold 720)       │

│ atr_multiplier      │ float    │ 0.5 – 5.0               │ 1.5                  │
│ atr_floor_pct      │ float    │ 0.0 – 2.0               │ 0.2-0.5              │
│ use_atr_floor      │ bool     │ true|false               │ true                  │
│ adx_threshold      │ float    │ 5 – 50                   │ 10-25                 │
│ vol_threshold_pct   │ float    │ 0.0 – 1.0               │ 0.10                 │
│ vol_max_pct        │ float    │ 0.0 – 2.0               │ 0.10-0.20            │
│ vol_min_pct        │ float    │ 0.0 – 1.0               │ 0.02-0.04            │
│ minimum_entry_quality│ float   │ 0.0 – 10.0              │ 0                     │
│ allow_bear_entries │ bool     │ true|false               │ see Section 8         │
└─────────────────────┴──────────┴───────────────────────────┴─────────────────────────┘
Malformed (out of range): validate_strategy_params raises -> reflection aborts that change.

C.5 goal.yaml  (reader: loop.load_goal, reflect; writer: _seed_default_state)
┌─────────────────────┬──────────┬───────────────────────────┬─────────────────────────┐
│ field               │ type     │ valid range               │ default               │
├─────────────────────┼──────────┼───────────────────────────┼─────────────────────────┤
│ pairs               │ list[str]│ pair names               │ forex/gold lists      │
│ target_return_30d   │ float    │ >0                       │ 0.05                 │
│ max_drawdown        │ float    │ 0<d<1                   │ 0.08                 │

│ failure_below      │ float    │ negative                 │ -0.04                │

│ gemini_reflection   │ bool     │ true                     │ true                  │

│ hypothesis_tracking │ bool     │ true                     │ true                  │
└─────────────────────┴──────────┴───────────────────────────┴─────────────────────────┘

C.6 discovered/{PAIR}.json  (writer: backtest.register_discovered_indicator /
   save_discovered_indicators; reader: backtest.load_discovered_indicators,
   loop.check_discovered_signals). Top-level = {"pair": str, "indicators": [...]}.
Per-indicator object (verbatim from backtest.py:1508):
  {
    "name":        "discovered_XXXX"   # random 1000-9999
    "_expr":       [OP, CHILD1, ...]   # the GP tree (nested list)
    "fitness":     float               # |OOS corr| at registration
    "expr_str":    str                 # _expr_to_str(_expr)
    "params":      {"threshold": 0.3}
    "total_pnl":   float (if fitness_details supplied)
    "win_rate":    float (if fitness_details supplied)
    "discovered_at":str (if supplied)
    "seed_run":    int   (if supplied)
    "status":      "shadow" | "live"   # shadow until promoted (B.4)
  }
  Malformed _expr (not a list / wrong op): _eval_tree returns [0.0]*len -> silently
  produces flat zero signal -> indicator contributes nothing (caught by degradation cull).

C.7 state/skips.json  (writer: loop.log_skip, loop.py:1302; reader: dashboard
   upsert_skips / skip-analysis endpoint). One object per line (skipped_signals.jsonl
   in forex; the blueprint Section 1.5 called it skips.json — actual file is
   skipped_signals.jsonl in STATE_DIR). Fields:
  { "ts","pair","bot_id":"hermes-forex","reason_skipped","rsi_at_skip","price_at_skip",
    "regime_at_skip","adx_at_skip","price_24h_later":null,"missed_pnl":null }
  Malformed: each skip appended atomically; reader splits on "\n" and json.loads each.

C.8 state/crisis_embeddings.json  (writer: crisis_learning._save_crises; reader:
   _load_crises). Top-level = dict keyed by crisis_id. Each entry:
  { "name":str, "signature":[[9 floats],...], "pnl_impact":float,
    "optimal_stop":float, "optimal_target":float, "regime":str,
    "volatility_class":str, ["pair":str, "ts":str for lived crises] }
  Signature vectors are LISTS OF 9-FLOAT VECTORS (one per timestep in the window).
  Malformed: _load_crises falls back to _DEFAULT_CRISES if file missing/unparseable.

C.9 state/chart_cache.json  (writer: chart_vision._set_cached_context; reader:
   _get_cached_context). One file per symbol: chart_ctx_{SYMBOL}.json:
  { "context": str, "ts": float (time.time()) }
  Cache TTL = ANALYSIS_INTERVAL (60 min). If time.time()-ts >= TTL -> miss.

C.10 state/allocation_matrix.json  (writer: gp_intelligence._update_matrix; reader:
   _condition_key consumers). Top-level = dict keyed by condition_key (B.6):
  { "EUR|BULL|trending|low|LDN": { "gp":{n,wins,pnl}, "traditional":{n,wins,pnl} }, ... }

C.11 POST /api/ingest/{bot_name}  (dashboard backend/main.py:292)
  Headers:  X-Ingest-Token: <INGEST_TOKEN>   (only if INGEST_TOKEN env set; else open)
  Body (JSON):  { "recent_trades":    [ <C.2 record>, ... ],
                  "recent_hypotheses":[ <C.3 record>, ... ],
                  "recent_skips":      [ <C.7 record>, ... ],
                  "strategy_json":     <strategy YAML as dict>,
                  "goal_json":         <goal.yaml as dict>,
                  "heartbeat_json":    <C.1 dict>,
                  "open_trades_json":  [ ... ] }
  Behaviour:
    - bot_name must be in VALID_BOTS else 404.
    - token mismatch -> 401.
    - missing body fields -> handled per-field (recent_* default to []).
    - writes latest_state (upsert) + appends trades/hypotheses/skips (upsert by PK).
  Response: {"status":"received","bot":bot_name,"ts":utcnow}

C.12 POST /api/ingest/{bot_name}/heartbeat  (NOT in current code — only the
   full ingest carries heartbeat_json). See C.11. If a standalone heartbeat endpoint
   is required in rebuild, it must update bot_status.desired_state + last_seen.

C.13 GET /api/trades/{bot_name}  (backend:486)
  Query params: pair (optional), limit (default 5000).
  Response: JSON list of trade rows (SQLite `trades` table columns, C.2 + raw_json).
  If pair given: filtered by pair. If limit given: capped.
  bot_name not in VALID_BOTS -> 404.

C.14 GET /api/summary/{bot_name}  (backend overview/summary)
  Response: { "bot":str, "total_pnl":float, "win_rate":float, "trades":int,
              "open_trades":int, "strategy_version":str, "last_update":str,
              "hypotheses":int, "skips":int, "crisis_status":dict }
  Exact keys depend on the summary builder; the overview endpoint aggregates all bots.

═══════════════════════════════════════════════════

APPENDIX D — BACKTEST PIPELINE COMPLETE SPECIFICATION
═══════════════════════════════════════════════════

> The 7-phase pipeline is invoked by reflect.validate_proposal -> backtest_with_history
> (lines ~486-660) for param changes, and by hermes_gp_cron for GP discoveries.
> Order: Phase 0 (OOS) FIRST — this was historically wrong (OOS was last) and is
> the single most important correctness fix (Section 9 lesson 8).

PHASE 0 — Out-of-Sample Validation (backtest_with_history, ~line 625)
  Input:        full price series (yfinance 6mo daily, or local 5m candles if >=5 files)
  Acceptance:   OOS delta > -0.2  (oOS_delta = oos_new.total_pnl - oos_old.total_pnl;
                oos = last 30% of series; oos_approved = oos_delta > -0.2)
  Rejection:    if oos_approved is False AND delta < 0.5 -> approved=False,
                reason "OOS FAIL: worsened in holdout set".
  Output:       approved (bool), reasons[]
  On fail:      returns {"approved":False,...} immediately; NO further phases run.
  State written: none (validation only).

PHASE 1 — Historical Backtest (sim() inside backtest_with_history, ~line 578)
  Input:        same series; old_params vs new_params (stop/target/trailing/time_exit)
  Acceptance:   delta = new_pnl - old_pnl > -0.1  (approved = delta > -0.1)
  Rejection:    if no entries simulated in window -> {"approved":True,"reason":"No entries"}
  Output:       old_result{total_pnl,wr,entries}, new_result{...}
  On partial fail: crisis/oos gates can still flip approved=False if delta<0.5.

PHASE 1.5 — Crisis Stress Test (crisis_backtest, ~line 403)
  Input:        price series, old/new params, strategy_type, threshold, pair
  Acceptance:    crisis_result.approved == True (no crisis window materially worse)
  Rejection:    if not approved AND delta < 0.5 -> approved=False,
                reason "CRISIS FAIL: <detail>"
  Output:       crisis_result{approved, reason}
  Note:         cross-references crisis_embeddings.json (B/C.8) for stop/target warnings.

PHASE 2 — Walk-Forward / Permutation (genetic_discovery._permutation_pvalue,
              _discover_walkforward)
  Input:        discovered indicator series + forward returns
  Acceptance:   permutation p-value significant (indicator beats random shuffles)
  Rejection:    p-value not significant -> indicator discarded
  Output:       p-value float
  Note:         for param changes this phase is represented by the OOS + alpha steps.

PHASE 3 — Alpha Decomposition (alpha_decomposition, ~line 678)
  Input:        trade list + candle series
  Acceptance:    alpha = skill component of returns (positive preferred)
  Rejection:    negative alpha signals luck, not skill (flagged, not hard-rejected)
  Output:       alpha_estimate (rounded 4dp, stored in trade record alpha_pct)

PHASE 4 — Regime Breakdown (_classify_regime, line 636)
  Input:        price series
  Output:       regime = BULL / BEAR / NEUTRAL (50/200 MA)
  Used by:      crisis cross-ref, allocation matrix bucketing.

PHASE 5 — Confidence + Correlation Risk (correlation_risk, ~line 698)
  Input:        list of suggested param changes across pairs
  Acceptance:   risk_flag == False (no correlated over-leverage across pairs)
  Rejection:    risk_flag True -> warning returned to reflection
  Output:       {risk_flag, groups_at_risk, detail}

PHASE 6 — Deploy / Register (save_yaml + register path)
  Input:        validated proposal
  Acceptance:   all prior phases approved
  Rejection:    any prior phase failed -> no deploy
  Output:       strategy YAML mutated; for GP: register_discovered_indicator + promote
  State written: strategy YAML file; discovered/{PAIR}.json; hypotheses.jsonl; cortex.

KEY NUMERIC GATES (verbatim):
  OOS:   oos_delta > -0.2 ; flips only if delta < 0.5
  Phase1: delta > -0.1
  Crisis: approved AND (delta >= 0.5 to survive a crisis fail)
  PnL report thresholds: improved if new-old > 0.5 ; worsened if old-new > 0.5
  GP cron: WR>=45% & PnL>0 (6mo); crisis WR>=40% (4 windows); |r|>0.8 reject;
           replace if best beats prior by >10% WR AND better PnL.

═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

APPENDIX E — LLM PROMPT TEMPLATES VERBATIM
═══════════════════════════════════════════════════

E.1 CHART VISION PROMPT (chart_vision.py:44, sent to Gemini PRIMARY + Groq FALLBACK)
─────────────────────────────────────────────
Reproduced verbatim from Appendix A (hermes-forex/hermes_forex/chart_vision.py, CHART_PROMPT).

```text
You are a professional technical analyst. Look at this price chart and return ONLY a JSON object with NO extra text. Format exactly:\n{"trend": "uptrend"|"downtrend"|"sideways", "confidence": 0.0-1.0, "sr_level": "support at X, resistance at Y", "recommendation": "enter long"|"wait for pullback"|"avoid entirely"}\nThe confidence value must reflect how certain you are about the trend direction. Brief and precise. NO markdown, NO explanation, ONLY the JSON object.
```

Hard-block logic (loop.py L14/L16): if parsed trend == "downtrend" or recommendation ==
"avoid entirely" -> HARD BLOCK entry. If recommendation == "enter long" is absent and
trend=="downtrend" -> soft filter when entry_quality<5.
Cache: 60 min (ANALYSIS_INTERVAL). Models: Gemini gemini-2.5-flash (PRIMARY),
Groq llama-4-scout-17b-16e-instruct (FALLBACK).

E.2 L2 REFLECTION PROMPT (reflect.py:747, sent to Gemini + Groq + DeepSeek consensus)
─────────────────────────────────────────────
Reproduced verbatim from Appendix A (hermes-forex/hermes_forex/reflect.py, layer2_gemini).

```text
You are a senior quantitative strategist at a proprietary trading firm. Your job is to preserve capital first, generate profit second.

ASSET: {symbol} | VERSION: {strategy.get('version', '?')}
PORTFOLIO CONTEXT: This pair has a score of {score['score']}/100 (guard threshold: 55). {"This pair is STRONG — approach with extreme caution." if score['score'] >= 55 else "This pair needs improvement — but one wrong change destroys weeks of progress."}

=== CURRENT STRATEGY (params you can adjust) ===
  stop_loss_pct:       {strategy.get('stop_loss_pct','?')}
  profit_target_pct:   {strategy.get('profit_target_pct','?')}
  entry.threshold:     {strategy.get('entry',{}).get('threshold','?')}
  trailing_stop_pct:   {strategy.get('trailing_stop_pct','?')}
  time_exit_cycles:    {strategy.get('time_exit_cycles','?')}
  position_size_r:     {strategy.get('position_size_r','?')}

=== PERFORMANCE OVERVIEW ===
  Total PnL:   {score['total_return']}% (target: {score['target_return']}%) | Avg PnL: {score['avg_pnl']}%
  Win rate:    {score['win_rate']:.0%} | Sharpe: {score['sharpe']} | Worst DD: {score['worst_dd']}%
  Exit breakdown: stop_loss={score['exit_reasons'].get('stop_loss', 0)} | profit_target={score['exit_reasons'].get('profit_target', 0)} | time_exit={score['exit_reasons'].get('time_exit', 0)} | trailing_stop={score['exit_reasons'].get('trailing_stop', 0)}

=== STRATEGY TYPE ===
  Current: {strategy.get('strategy_type', 'mean_reversion')}
  Valid types: mean_reversion, rsi_momentum
{force_strategy_text}
LAST {len(trade_lines)} TRADES:
{trades_text}

=== PATTERN ANALYSIS ===
Exit breakdown: {score['exit_reasons'].get('stop_loss', 0)} stops, {score['exit_reasons'].get('profit_target', 0)} targets, {score['exit_reasons'].get('time_exit', 0)} timeouts
Time-exit rate: {score['time_exit_pct']:.0%} (above 30% means target is too ambitious)
Session breakdown: {session_analysis}
Day-of-week: {day_text}
Entry regime: {regime_text}
Regime changes during hold: {flip_text}
Alpha (luck vs skill): {alpha_text}

=== PER-VERSION HISTORY ===
{versions_text}

{"CHART: " + chart_context[:300] if chart_context else ""}
SKIPPED SIGNALS: {skipped_json[:1000]}
{crisis_context}
{cortex_context}

=== REASONING PROTOCOL — FOLLOW IN ORDER ===

WARNING: Every param change carries real PnL risk. This system lost 60pp of win rate on one bad change. Only recommend a change if the current strategy is CLEARLY failing, never for marginal improvement.

Step 1 — DIAGNOSE the root cause (choose ONE):
  a) WRONG ENTRIES: entries not in oversold territory (RSI too high), or wrong session/regime
  b) WRONG EXITS: stops firing correctly on bad entries (entry problem, NOT stop problem) vs stops too tight for normal vol
  c) WRONG STRATEGY TYPE: pair doesn't behave like mean_reversion or rsi_momentum
  d) TIMING: losses cluster in one session or one day of week
  e) NO CLEAR ISSUE: performance is normal variance

Step 2 — CITE EVIDENCE (which specific trades support the diagnosis)
  Reference trade details: PnL, entry RSI, session, exit reason

Step 3 — RECOMMEND (one param change maximum, usually none)
  • If stops fire but RSI@entry is not deeply oversold (<35): tighten entry.threshold (not widen stop)
  • If stops fire and RSI@entry IS oversold (<35): stop might be too tight, consider widening 0.3-0.5%
  • If time exits dominate (>30%): tighten entry.threshold or lower profit_target_pct
  • If all losses are in one session: session filter is more appropriate than param change
  • If alpha is negative but win rate is high: entries are luck, not skill — tighten conditions
  • If alpha is positive but PnL is flat: exits are wrong, consider longer time_exit or trailing

Step 4 — IDENTIFY RISK (what could go wrong with this change)

=== OUTPUT FORMAT ===
PARAM: <param_name or "none">
OLD_VALUE: <current value>
NEW_VALUE: <new value>
CONFIDENCE: <high/medium/low>
REASONING: <2-3 sentences: diagnosis first, then recommendation, then risk>
CONSENSUS_CHECK: <what would the other models miss about this pair?>
```

Consensus: call_llm_consensus(prompt) runs Gemini + Groq(DeepSeek distill) + DeepSeek;
2/3 vote selects param; reasoning merged. Valid params limited to:
  entry.threshold, strategy_type, stop_loss_pct, profit_target_pct,
  trailing_stop_pct, time_exit_cycles, position_size_r.
Confidence gate: conf >= 0.40 to apply (L50). Score gate: score >= 55 to reach L2 (L41).

E.3 BACKTEST LLM VALIDATION PROMPT
  Function: _llm_validate_backtest
  File: hermes-forex/hermes_forex/reflect.py (source in Appendix A)
  Model: DeepSeek (primary) -> Gemini (fallback) -> Groq (fallback)
    [Exact model strings from env: DEEPSEEK_MODEL default "deepseek-chat";
     GEMINI_MODEL default "gemini-2.5-pro"; GROQ_MODEL default
     "deepseek-r1-distill-llama-70b". Cascade shown at reflect.py:5290-5303.]
  Verbatim prompt string (reproduced exactly from Appendix A reflect.py):

```python
prompt = f"""You are a senior quantitative strategist at a proprietary trading firm.

A backtest engine has evaluated a proposed parameter change and the result is MARGINAL (score={score or '?'}). 
The mechanical heuristic cannot decide clearly. You must make the final call.

CHANGE: {pair} {param}: {old_val} -> {new_val}

RECENT TRADES:
{trades_text}

{regime_text}
{alpha_text}

RULES:
- NEVER approve a stop widening unless stops are hitting on oversold RSI (<35)
- NEVER approve a target reduction unless time-exits dominate (>40%)
- If regime data shows the change helps in BULL but hurts in BEAR, REJECT (asymmetric risk)
- If alpha is strongly negative AND win rate is high, the strategy is lucky not skilled — REJECT
- If this change has already been rejected by crisis or OOS validation, REJECT
- INVALID changes (no benefit, unclear benefit) = REJECT. Only approve clear improvements.

Respond with EXACTLY one line:
APPROVE: <2 sentence reasoning>
REJECT: <2 sentence reasoning>"""
```

  Variables injected (in order of appearance):
  | {variable_name} | Source | Type | Example value |
  |-----------------|--------|------|---------------|
  | {score}         | param (score=None default) | str/None | "0.62" |
  | {pair}          | function arg | str | "EUR/USD" |
  | {param}         | function arg | str | "stop_loss_pct" |
  | {old_val}       | function arg | str | "1.5" |
  | {new_val}       | function arg | str | "2.0" |
  | {trades_text}   | built from trades[-5:] | str | "  PnL=+1.20% exit=profit_target RSI=34 regime=BULL" |
  | {regime_text}   | built if regime_bd | str | "Regimes: BULL: 3t avg+0.500%" |
  | {alpha_text}    | built if alpha_info | str | "Alpha: skill=+0.200% luck=-0.100%" |

  Response parsing: first line upper-stripped; startswith("APPROVE") -> {"approved": True,...};
  startswith("REJECT") -> {"approved": False,...}; else {"approved": None,...}.

[APPENDIX E VERIFIED — chart vision prompt (E.1) and L2 reflection prompt (E.2) reproduced
verbatim from Appendix A source; backtest LLM validation prompt (E.3) reproduced verbatim
from reflect.py. All three prompts are copy-pasteable.]

E.4 CRISIS LEARNING PROMPT
  NONE. crisis_learning.py uses NO LLM. It is pure vector math (cosine distance +
  pre-seeded signatures). get_crisis_recommendation returns a dict directly.

E.5 SELF-AUDIT PROMPT
  NONE. self_audit.py is pure static analysis (file checks + API pings). No LLM call.

E.6 OTHER PROMPTS — L1 RULE-BASED (reflect.layer1_rule_based)
  Not an LLM prompt — deterministic rule engine. Returns (variable, old, new, reason,
  confidence) from a fixed decision tree (no free-text prompt).

APPENDIX F — CRISIS LEARNING COMPLETE SPECIFICATION
═══════════════════════════════════════════════════

F.1 THE 9 CRISIS SIGNATURE DIMENSIONS (exact, crisis_learning.py:30 comment + _extract_crisis_features:218)
  Feature order index:
    [0] atr_pct_5m      = ATR(14) / last_price * 100          (5-minute volatility %)
    [1] atr_pct_1h      = atr_pct_5m * 0.6 (approximated 1h ATR %)
    [2] rsi/100          = RSI(14) / 100.0
    [3] adx/100          = 0.3 (approximated ADX, hard-coded placeholder)
    [4] vol_ratio        = (avg vol last 3) / (avg vol last 12)  [volume surge ratio]
    [5] spread_proxy     = atr_pct_5m * 0.3 (approximated spread %)
    [6] session_hour     = datetime.now(utc).hour / 23.0
    [7] day_of_week      = datetime.now(utc).weekday() / 6.0
    [8] price_ma_ratio   = last_price / SMA(50)
  All values rounded to 4-6 dp. Signature = LIST of these 9-vectors, one per sampled
  timestep in the crisis window (CRISIS_WINDOW_SIZE=12, sampled every 5 cycles).

F.2 DISTANCE FUNCTION (exact, _crisis_signature_distance:108)
  Distance metric = COSINE DISTANCE.
  For each timestep vector `sig_vec` in the stored crisis signature:
      nf  = L2_normalize(features)         # _normalize_vector
      ns  = L2_normalize(sig_vec)
      dot = sum(nf[i]*ns[i] for i) ; clamp dot to [-1,1]
      d   = 1.0 - dot                    # cosine distance
  Return = MEAN of per-timestep distances (averaged across the crisis window).
  If signature empty or length mismatch -> return 1.0 (max distance / "novel").

F.3 NOVEL REGIME THRESHOLD (exact)
  get_crisis_recommendation (line 153): if dist > 0.5 -> return None (too far from any
  known crisis = NOVEL regime, no recommendation applied).
  Loop flatline trigger (loop.py L21): novelty_score > 3.0 * median -> pause 60 cycles.
  (The 0.5 cosine-distance threshold is the "known crisis" boundary; >0.5 = novel.)

F.4 crisis_embeddings.json FORMAT (exact, C.8)
  { crisis_id: {
      "name": str,
      "signature": [ [9 floats], [9 floats], ... ],   # list of timestep vectors
      "pnl_impact": float,
      "optimal_stop": float,
      "optimal_target": float,
      "regime": str,
      "volatility_class": str,
      "pair": str (lived only),
      "ts": str (lived only, isoformat)
    }, ... }
  Pre-seeded ids: "covid_crash_2020", "nfp_spike_2023", "london_open_volatility".

F.5 ONLINE LEARNING SAVE (exact, save_lived_crisis:231)
  Triggered: after a flatline event resolves (loop calls it with pair, pnl_impact, histories).
  Requires: price_history >= 60 candles.
  Builds signature via _compute_crisis_signature_from_history (samples 12 windows, step =
  len//CRISIS_SAMPLE_INTERVAL(5); needs >=3 valid vectors else abort).
  crisis_id = f"lived_{pair}_{int(time.time())}"
  Classification: is_survived = pnl_impact > -5.0
  Writes:
      optimal_stop   = 1.5 if survived else 2.5
      optimal_target = 0.3 if not survived else 0.5
      regime = "LEARNED", volatility_class = "survived"/"danger"
      pair, ts recorded.

F.6 "FLATLINE" NUMERIC DEFINITION (exact)
  Two distinct flatline triggers in loop.py:
    (a) NOVEL-REGIME flatline (L21): novelty_score > 3.0 * median_novelty ->
        pause pair for 60 cycles.
    (b) CONSECUTIVE-LOSS flatline (L22, _update_consecutive_losses:1076):
        N consecutive losing exits (stop_loss) -> flatline for a computed number of cycles
        (cons_cycles). Exact N is the bot's configured consecutive-loss limit (see loop
        constant; historically 3-5). On trigger, _flatline[pair] = cycle + cons_cycles.
  During flatline: pair is skipped for entries; heartbeat/flatline_log.jsonl records reason.

F.7 CRISIS → REFLECTION INJECTION (exact)
  crisis_data is passed into combined_reflect -> layer2_gemini as {crisis_context}
  (the f-string {crisis_context} in the L2 prompt, E.2). It is built by loop from
  crisis_learning.get_crisis_recommendation(features) and appended to the prompt so the
  LLM sees the recommended stop/target during a known/near crisis.
