│ NEWS_API_KEY            │ gold adapters  │ newsapi key (DEAD in gold)  │ YES    │ (set in secrets; not used)      │
│ DISCORD_ALERTS_WEBHOOK  │ all bots       │ trade/flatline/audit alerts │ YES    │ Discord webhook URL              │
│ DISCORD_REFLECTIONS_    │ reflect        │ reflection-output webhook    │ YES    │ Discord webhook URL              │
│  WEBHOOK               │                │                              │        │                                  │
│ DISCORD_DAILY_WEBHOOK  │ loop/reflect  │ daily report webhook        │ YES    │ Discord webhook URL              │
│ DISCORD_EXILE_WEBHOOK  │ decision_cortex│ exile/reinstate alerts     │ YES    │ Discord webhook URL              │
│ INGEST_TOKEN            │ dashboard      │ auth token for /api/ingest   │ YES    │ "" (if empty, ingest is open)   │
│ DB_PATH                 │ dashboard      │ SQLite file path            │ no     │ /data/hermes.db                │
│ PORT                    │ dashboard      │ uvicorn listen port         │ no     │ 8000                            │
│ RAILWAY_TOKEN           │ dashboard/cron │ Railway API token (redeploy)│ YES    │ "" (used for bot toggle redeploy)│
│ RAILWAY_ENVIRONMENT_ID  │ dashboard      │ Railway service env id      │ no     │ 952f0a72-... (hard-coded ex.)  │
│ MPLBACKEND              │ chart_vision  │ matplotlib backend          │ no     │ "Agg" (headless PNG render)     │
└─────────────────────────────────┴──────────────────┴───────────────────────────────┴────────┴──────────────────────────────────────┘

Notes:
  - Internal flags DISCORD_WEBHOOKS_LOADED / DISCORD_WEBHOOK_CHECKED are NOT env vars
    (they are in-process caches). Do not list them as config.
  - The dashboard VALID_BOTS set must equal the union of HERMES_BOT_NAME values across
    the three bots (forex, gold, crypto) to avoid the stale-trade contamination bug (P5/H.7).

═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

APPENDIX H — DASHBOARD API COMPLETE CONTRACT
═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

H.1 ENDPOINTS (backend/main.py)
┌──────────┬─────────────────────────────────┬───────────────────────────────────┐
│ Method   │ Path                            │ Description                   │
├──────────┼─────────────────────────────────┼───────────────────────────────────┤
│ GET      │ /api/health                    │ liveness                     │
│ GET      │ /api/spark?pair=&bars=30      │ sparkline OHLCV              │
│ POST     │ /api/ingest/{bot_name}        │ bot pushes full state (C.11) │
│ GET      │ /api/bot/{bot_name}/pulse     │ desired_state + last_seen    │
│ POST     │ /api/bot/{bot_name}/toggle    │ flip running/stopped (Railway)│
│ GET      │ /api/overview                  │ all-bot aggregate           │
│ GET      │ /api/bot/{bot_name}/trades    │ trade history (C.13)        │
│ GET      │ /api/alerts                   │ list alerts                 │
│ POST     │ /api/alerts/dismiss           │ dismiss alert               │
│ GET      │ /api/daily-summary            │ daily PnL summary          │
│ GET      │ /api/lifetime-summary         │ lifetime summary            │
│ GET      │ /api/range-summary            │ date-range summary          │
│ GET      │ /api/skip-analysis/{bot_name}  │ skipped-signal analysis     │
│ GET      │ /api/strategy-params/{bot_name}│ current strategy params    │
│ GET      │ /api/detailed-report/{bot_name}│ full report               │
│ GET      │ /api/export-text              │ export all as text          │
│ GET      │ /api/export-text/pair/{pair}  │ export one pair as text     │
│ GET      │ /api/auth/status              │ auth status                 │
│ POST     │ /api/auth/setup              │ set password                │
│ POST     │ /api/auth/login              │ login                       │
│ GET      │ /api/auth/verify             │ verify token                │
│ POST     │ /api/chat                    │ LLM chat (ChatBody)        │
│ GET      │ /api/per-version/{bot_name}   │ per-version stats           │
└──────────┴─────────────────────────────────┴───────────────────────────────────┘
H.2 REQUEST BODIES (POST)
  /api/ingest/{bot_name}:  see C.11 (JSON: recent_trades, recent_hypotheses,
      recent_skips, strategy_json, goal_json, heartbeat_json, open_trades_json).
      Header X-Ingest-Token required if INGEST_TOKEN set.
  /api/auth/setup:    {"password": str}
  /api/auth/login:    {"password": str}  -> returns token
  /api/alerts/dismiss:{"alert_key": str}
  /api/chat:          ChatBody schema (message, history) -> LLM response
H.3 RESPONSE SCHEMAS (GET) — see C.13 / C.14 for trades & summary.
  /api/overview: {bots:[{bot,total_pnl,win_rate,trades,open_trades,strategy_version,
      last_update}], hypotheses, skips, crisis_status}
  /api/bot/{bot}/pulse: {bot, desired_state, last_seen?}
H.4 AUTHENTICATION
  Simple password auth. /api/auth/setup sets a password (stored hashed in app_config
  table). /api/auth/login returns a token (auth_tokens table). Protected routes check
  the token. Ingest is protected by X-Ingest-Token (separate from user auth).
H.5 SQLITE TABLE SCHEMAS (verbatim DDL, backend:81-157)
  trades(bot TEXT, id TEXT, pair TEXT, entry_price REAL, exit_price REAL, entry_ts TEXT,
         exit_ts TEXT, pnl_pct REAL, exit_reason TEXT, hold_cycles INTEGER,
         entry_rsi REAL, entry_regime TEXT, entry_quality_score REAL,
         strategy_type TEXT, chart_context TEXT, raw_json TEXT,
         PRIMARY KEY(bot, id))
  hypotheses(bot TEXT, ts TEXT, pair TEXT, version_from TEXT, version_to TEXT,
         variable TEXT, old_value TEXT, new_value TEXT, reasoning TEXT, mode TEXT,
         raw_json TEXT, PRIMARY KEY(bot, ts, variable))
  skips(bot TEXT, ts TEXT, pair TEXT, reason_skipped TEXT, rsi_at_skip REAL,
        price_at_skip REAL, missed_pnl REAL, raw_json TEXT,
        PRIMARY KEY(bot, ts, pair))
  latest_state(bot TEXT PRIMARY KEY, strategy_json TEXT, goal_json TEXT,
         heartbeat_json TEXT, open_trades_json TEXT DEFAULT '[]', received_at TEXT)
  dismissed_alerts(alert_key TEXT PRIMARY KEY, dismissed_at TEXT)
  bot_status(bot TEXT PRIMARY KEY, desired_state TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'running',
         updated_at TEXT)
  auth_tokens(token TEXT PRIMARY KEY, created_at TEXT, expires_at TEXT)
  app_config(key TEXT PRIMARY KEY, value TEXT)
  Migration: ALTER TABLE latest_state ADD COLUMN open_trades_json TEXT DEFAULT '[]'
H.6 bot_name SEPARATION
  Every row is keyed by `bot` (from URL path {bot_name}). VALID_BOTS whitelist
  (forex, gold, crypto) rejects unknown bots with 404. Trades/hypotheses/skips use
  PRIMARY KEY (bot, ...) so forex and gold rows never collide.
H.7 STALE-TRADE CONTAMINATION BUG (root cause + inconsistent field)
  ROOT CAUSE: the `raw_json` column is only updated on INSERT/ON CONFLICT update
  (upsert_trades lines 197-203 updates exit_price/exit_ts/pnl_pct/exit_reason/
  hold_cycles only — NOT raw_json). If a bot pushes under the WRONG bot_name (e.g.
  both forex and gold accidentally use "forex"), their trades land in the same
  (bot,id) key space and silently overwrite each other; if bot_name mismatches the
  loop's HERMES_BOT_NAME, the dashboard shows one bot's trades under another.
  INCONSISTENT FIELD: `pair` is written from t.get("pair") or t.get("asset") at ingest
  (line 206), but the loop writes BOTH "pair" and "asset" (identical). Some older
  trade records only had "asset" -> the dashboard's pair filter (C.13) can miss them
  because it queries the `pair` column. The fix: always populate BOTH pair and asset
  identically, and enforce that HERMES_BOT_NAME == dashboard VALID_BOTS entry.

═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

APPENDIX I — SESSION FILTER COMPLETE SPECIFICATION
═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

I.1 SESSION WINDOWS (exact UTC, from loop._get_session:601 and is_*_session funcs)
  London (LDN):  08:00 <= hour < 17:00 UTC   (function returns "LDN")
  New York (NY):  13:00 <= hour < 21:00 UTC   (function returns "NY")
  Asia (ASIA):     00:00 <= hour < 08:00 UTC   (function returns "ASIA")
  Other:           hour >= 21 or (hour>=17 and hour<21? actually 17-21 is "OTHER")
                   -> _get_session returns "OTHER" for 17:00-23:59 and 21:00-23:59.
  NOTE: There is a GAP/overlap quirk: NY ends at 21:00, LD移植N ends at 17:00, so
  17:00-21:00 returns "OTHER" (not NY). london_only pairs trade 08-17; ny_only
  trade 13-21; asian_only trade 00-08; 24h trades always.

I.2 APPLIES TO ENTRIES ONLY (not exits)
  The session filter is checked ONLY at ENTRY (loop L04 session guard, lines ~1929-1937).
  Open trades are exited by stop/target/time regardless of session. A trade opened in
  session stays open through session close.

I.3 SIGNAL ONE CYCLE BEFORE CLOSE
  The entry check uses the CURRENT UTC hour at the moment of the cycle. If a signal
  fires at 16:59:59 UTC for a london_only pair, it is TAKEN (16:59 < 17:00).
  At 17:00:00 exactly, _get_session returns "OTHER" -> london_only mismatch -> SKIPPED.
  So: 16:59:59 = taken; 17:00:00 = skipped. The boundary is the integer hour
  comparison `8 <= hour < 17` (hour is datetime.now(utc).hour, an int 0-23).

I.4 DAYLIGHT SAVING TIME
  All comparisons use datetime.now(timezone.utc).hour — UTC, NOT local. DST does NOT
  affect the filter because the source of truth is UTC hour. (DST only matters if the
  bot host clock is wrong; the code explicitly uses UTC.)

I.5 BANK HOLIDAYS
  NOT handled. The session filter is purely hour-based; bank holidays (e.g. Christmas
  London closed) are NOT special-cased. A london_only pair will still attempt entries
  at 08-17 UTC on a London bank holiday. (No holiday calendar exists in code.)

I.6 EXACT FUNCTION + FILE (verified)
  Function: _get_session(hour:int) -> str   in hermes-forex/hermes_forex/loop.py:601
  Helpers:  is_ny_session()     loop.py:1268 (0<=h<21? actually 13<=h<21)
            is_london_session() loop.py (8<=h<17)
            is_asian_session()  loop.py:1271 (0<=h<8)
  The strategy YAML session_filter value maps: "london_only"->LDN, "ny_only"->NY,
  "asian_only"->ASIA, "24h"->always allowed.

I.7 EDGE CASES (london_only)
  08:00:00 UTC -> hour=8 -> 8<=8<17 TRUE -> SESSION OK (entry allowed).
  08:00:01 UTC -> hour=8 -> same -> allowed.
  17:00:00 UTC -> hour=17 -> 8<=17<17 FALSE -> SKIPPED.
  16:59:59 UTC -> hour=16 -> allowed.
  (The minute/second do not matter — only the integer UTC hour is compared.)

═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

APPENDIX J — SELF-AUDIT ENGINE COMPLETE SPECIFICATION
═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

J.1 SUBSYSTEMS AUDITED (self_audit.py, 8 audit methods + run())
  1. audit_reflection()   — checks trades.jsonl exists, >=3 trades (data-quality gate),
                            hypotheses scored/unscored, cooldown (.cooldown.json) active.
  2. audit_gp_entries()   — reads gp_entries.jsonl: win/loss track, per-indicator WR;
                            flags <40% WR over >=5 entries as CRITICAL; reads gp_lockout.json
                            (locked pairs), gp_indicator_stats.json (<30% WR -> CRITICAL).
  3. audit_degradation()   — reads discovered/_live_deg.json: empty/culled/near-cull
                            (count>=40 & wr<0.4).
  4. audit_discovery()     — counts discovered/*.json indicator files + total indicators.
  5. audit_dashboard()    — HTTP GET /api/bot/forex/pulse (desired_state==running?),
                            /api/discovered (200?), POST /api/ingest/forex (500/401/422?).
  6. audit_persistence()   — state dir exists; trades.jsonl + strategies present;
                            volume writable (.audit_write_test).
  7. audit_correction_chain()— gp_lockout.json persisted? _live_deg.json present &
                            has >=50 signals? weekly cron expected.
  8. audit_cortex()       — imports decision_cortex.summary(); exiled indicators,
                            per-type WR gaps (MR>=50% & GP<30% => warn), near-exile count,
                            total entries ==0 => warn.
  9. audit_policy()       — get_policy(): active suppressions, allocation active,
                            priority discovery (2+ exiled), rollback candidates, probe_interval.

J.2 SCHEDULE
  run() is invoked on-demand (python -m hermes_forex.self_audit) or as a weekly cron.
  The prompt's "daily 08:00 UTC" in Section 10 checklist is NOT what the code does —
  the code has NO internal scheduler; it is a CLI / cron-invoked script. The
  CHANGELOG references a daily self-audit but the engine itself runs when called.
  CORRECTION: treat self_audit as "run on demand or via external cron" — NOT an
  in-loop 08:00 daily timer in the current code.

J.3 ACTIONS ON PROBLEM
  Self-audit is REPORT-ONLY. It does NOT auto-fix or pause trading. It appends
  findings to self.findings with severity CRITICAL / WARNING / INFO and prints a
  structured JSON report {timestamp, summary, critical, warnings, info, findings}.
  The caller (cron or operator) decides whether to act. No trades are modified.

J.4 FEEDBACK INTO OTHER ENGINES
  Self-audit does NOT write to any engine state. It READS:
    - trades.jsonl, hypotheses.jsonl, skipped_signals.jsonl (state)
    - gp_entries.jsonl, gp_lockout.json, gp_indicator_stats.json (state)
    - discovered/_live_deg.json, discovered/*.json (state)
    - cortex (via decision_cortex.summary import)
    - policy (via policy_engine.get_policy import)
    - dashboard API (HTTP)
  It emits only a report. Remediation is manual/operator-driven.

J.5 DISCORD ALERTS FROM SELF-AUDIT
  Self-audit itself sends NO Discord alerts. It is silent except for stdout/JSON.
  (Discord alerts for exile/reinstate come from decision_cortex._notify_exile /
  _notify_reinstatement; flatline/trade alerts come from loop; audit findings are
  surfaced by the external cron operator, not the engine.)

J.6 PRODUCTION TRIGGER HISTORY
  The self-audit engine has NOT been observed firing in production with findings in the
  provided state (state files are empty on disk: trades.jsonl=0 lines, hypotheses=0,
  discovered dirs empty). It has never reported a CRITICAL in the available logs.
  This is expected: with no trades yet, the audit correctly reports INFO/WARNING only.
  It is a DETECTION tool, validated by code review, not by a production incident.
